items.headline: U.S. Stocks Retreat Amid Inflation Fears and AI Disruption
items.summary: U.S. stocks experienced a broad sell-off on Friday, February 27, 2026, driven by persistent investor concerns over a surprisingly discouraging inflation report and continued apprehension regarding the impact of artificial intelligence on traditional businesses. Wall Street continued to penalize companies perceived as potential losers in the AI revolution. The S&P 500 concluded what is likely its second losing month in the last ten, while the Dow managed to secure its tenth consecutive month of gains, a streak not seen since early 2018. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures each fell 0.4%, Dow Jones futures sank 0.6%.

items.headline: Block Inc. Surges on Workforce Reduction
items.summary: Shares of Block Inc. ($SQ), the company behind Cash App and Square, jumped 16.8% on February 27, 2026, after CEO Jack Dorsey announced plans to cut 40% of its workforce, emphasizing a shift towards becoming an 'intelligence-native company' driven by AI tools.

items.headline: Netflix Up After Abandoning Acquisition Bid
items.summary: Netflix ($NFLX) shares rose 7.3% in premarket trading on February 27, 2026, after the streaming giant withdrew its bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming business.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Jumps on AI Server Demand
items.summary: Dell Technologies ($DELL) surged 21.8% on February 27, 2026, driven by strong demand for its AI-optimized servers and the announcement of a substantial share buyback program.

items.headline: Nvidia Extends Slide on AI Spending Concerns
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) experienced a 4.1% drop on February 27, 2026, as skepticism continued to grow regarding the long-term sustainability of significant AI capital expenditures by major technology firms.

items.headline: Apollo Global Management Falls on Private Credit Fears
items.summary: Apollo Global Management ($APO) declined 8.6% on February 27, 2026, amidst concerns of private credit contagion impacting financial heavyweights.

items.headline: Salesforce Declines on AI Disruption Worries
items.summary: Salesforce ($CRM) fell 3.7% on February 27, 2026, as investors expressed renewed worries that AI tools could disrupt traditional software business models, impacting demand for services.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Underway: Defensive and 'Old Economy' Outperform Tech
items.summary: A significant sector rotation is observed in early 2026, with defensive sectors such as consumer staples, healthcare, and utilities outperforming as investors seek safer havens. Simultaneously, 'old economy' sectors including basic materials, industrials, and energy are gaining favor, showing strong gains. Conversely, the technology sector, particularly chips and software, is weighing on indices due to lingering AI disruption fears and cooling euphoria following its strong 2025 performance.

items.headline: Hotter-Than-Expected Inflation Report Dampens Market Sentiment
items.summary: A surprisingly discouraging inflation update on February 27, 2026, revealed that wholesale inflation reached 2.9% last month, significantly exceeding economists' expectation of 1.6%. This hotter-than-expected Producer Price Index reinforced market expectations that the U.S. Federal Reserve is unlikely to implement interest rate cuts in the immediate future, dampening overall market sentiment.

items.headline: Fed Poised to Hold Rates Amid Economic Data
items.summary: Market participants are pricing in a high probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current benchmark interest rate at the upcoming March meeting. The Fed had already held rates steady in the 3.5% to 3.75% range at its January meeting, with strategists noting low odds for a rate cut in March.

items.headline: Oil Prices Climb Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices surged on February 27, 2026, with U.S. benchmark crude rising 2.8% to settle at $67.02 per barrel. The increase was attributed to heightened worries regarding tensions between the United States and Iran over Iran's nuclear program, signaling ongoing geopolitical risks impacting energy markets.

items.headline: Wilmar International Reports Strong 2H2025 Performance
items.summary: Agribusiness giant Wilmar International (SGX: F34) announced a robust 38.3% surge in its net profit for the second half of 2025, reaching US$815.9 million. This strong performance was primarily bolstered by improved crushing margins within its Feed and Industrial Products segment and gains from sugar merchandising.
